---
description: 'Use when reviewing code, checking a PR, auditing changes, or performing a code review on this project. Covers project-specific gotchas, patterns to enforce, and anti-patterns to flag.'
---

# Code Review Checklist – CSV Tools

## Routing

- [ ] **Never edit `src/routeTree.gen.ts` manually.** Flag any direct edits to this file — it is auto-generated by TanStack Router's Vite plugin.
- [ ] New routes must use `createFileRoute` in a file under `src/routes/`. The path string must match the filename exactly.

## Imports

- [ ] All imports must use the `@/` path alias. Flag any relative imports (`../`, `../../`) introduced in new or modified files.
- [ ] Dynamic `import()` calls must also use `@/` (e.g. `await import('@/utils/schema-builder')`).

## TypeScript

- [ ] **Do not flag missing `import React` or `import * as React`.** This project uses React 19 with the new JSX transform (`"jsx": "react-jsx"`). Named imports from `'react'` (e.g. `useState`, `useRef`) are sufficient. `React.ChangeEvent`, `React.DragEvent`, and other React types are available as globals — no namespace import needed.
- [ ] Generic type parameters must match `/^(T|T[A-Z][A-Za-z]+)$/`. Reject bare `K`, `V`, `T1`, etc.
- [ ] CSV row values typed as `Record<string, string>` — must be `Record<string, string | undefined>` since PapaParse can return undefined for missing columns.
- [ ] No `any` casts without justification. `as any` in route updates is only acceptable inside `routeTree.gen.ts`.

## Tailwind / Styling

- [ ] CSS custom properties must use shorthand syntax: `text-(--sea-ink)` not `text-[var(--sea-ink)]`.
- [ ] New design tokens must be added to both `:root` and `:root[data-theme='dark']` blocks in `src/styles.css`, and the `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)` block.

## Validation (Valibot)

- [ ] Schema building logic belongs in `src/utils/schema-builder.ts`, not inside components or routes.
- [ ] New `ColumnType` values must be handled in both `buildColumnValidator` (schema-builder) and `COLUMN_TYPES` (SchemaBuilder component).
- [ ] `validateRows` receives `Record<string, string | undefined>[]` — guard falsy values before passing to Valibot validators.

## i18n

- [ ] Any user-visible string hardcoded in JSX must be extracted to `messages/en.json` and `messages/es.json`. Both files must have the same keys.
- [ ] Run `npm run codegen` after adding new message keys.

## Feature Structure

- [ ] Feature-scoped components belong under `src/features/<feature>/components/`. Only truly shared layout goes in `src/components/`.
- [ ] No cross-feature imports (e.g. a `validator` component importing from a `billing` feature).

## Tests

- [ ] All test files live in `tests/`. No `*.test.ts` files inside `src/`.
- [ ] Integration tests that read sample files must use `import.meta.dirname` relative to `tests/` (one level up: `../samples`).
- [ ] New `ColumnType` entries or new constraint options need corresponding test coverage in `tests/schema-builder.test.ts`.

## Security

- [ ] File input accepts only `.csv` / `text/csv`. Reject attempts to broaden the `accept` attribute.

## Accessibility

- [ ] **Flag `role="button"` on a `<div>` or `<span>`.** Use `<button type="button">` instead — it handles Enter, Space, focus, and cursor natively.
- [ ] **Flag `role="group"` on a `<div>` wrapping form controls.** Use `<fieldset>` + `<legend>` — that is the semantic HTML equivalent.
- [ ] **Flag floating `htmlFor`/`id` label pairs on inputs/selects.** Prefer wrapping labels (`<label><span>Label text</span><input/></label>`) — same association, no IDs needed.
- [ ] **Flag missing `aria-hidden={true}` on decorative Lucide icons** when the icon's meaning is conveyed by adjacent visible text.
- [ ] **Flag `aria-live`, `role="status"`, or `role="alert"` added when a semantic HTML element would suffice.** These are appropriate only for dynamically injected content AT users would otherwise miss.
- [ ] Do not flag the absence of `aria-label` on elements that already have visible text content or a `<legend>`/`<label>` association.

## Cloudflare Workers Compatibility

- [ ] No Node.js built-ins (`fs`, `path`, `crypto`) in `src/`. These are only acceptable in `tests/` under Vitest's Node environment.
- [ ] Bundle size matters — avoid importing entire libraries when only specific functions are needed, especially for Valibot (tree-shake by importing named functions).
